key_strengths: QuickBooks holds ~80% US SMB accounting market share with structural switching costs and accountant network effects — one of the widest moats in software
Fortress balance sheet: 0.5x net debt/EBITDA, 20x interest coverage, $4.6B cash, zero survival risk in any scenario
Expanding operating leverage: margins grew from 20.1% to 26.2% over four years with revenue compounding at ~14% CAGR
Highly recurring revenue (~75-80% subscription) with predictable tax-cycle seasonality and diversified customer base of millions
key_risks: SBC of $2.0B (51% of net income, 10.6% of revenue) materially overstates real shareholder economics vs headline metrics
Current price requires ~18% annual SBC-adjusted owner earnings growth for a decade — demanding and unproven
AI is both opportunity and threat: could widen moat or commoditize tax prep and lower barriers for competitors
Revenue growth decelerating from 17% to 10% guided for Q3 FY2026
IRS Direct File and vertical SaaS competitors pose long-term threats to TurboTax and QuickBooks respectively
red_flags: CEO has made zero open-market share purchases in 5 years while selling $26.6M in January 2026
Mailchimp ($12B acquisition) showing revenue stagnation — largest capital allocation decision has underperformed
Negative margin of safety on SBC-adjusted conservative IV (-141%)
Buyback program partly offsets dilution rather than returning genuine capital — net share reduction only ~2% over 3 years despite $6.7B in buybacks
buy_triggers: Price declines to $250-300 range, bringing SBC-adjusted owner earnings yield above 4%
SBC/revenue ratio drops below 8%, demonstrating real per-share economics improvement
AI monetization shows up in measurable ARPU expansion and accelerating growth above 15%
sell_triggers: SBC/revenue rises above 13% with no plan to moderate
Revenue growth drops below 5% for two consecutive quarters
Major competitive threat materializes (government free filing revival, tech platform entry) and TurboTax ARPU declines
valuation_summary: At $447, INTU is fairly valued on non-GAAP metrics (16.9x forward P/E) but expensive on SBC-adjusted owner earnings (2.1% yield, ~48x P/OE). Conservative IV of $185, base $265, bull $353 — all below current price. The 45% drawdown from highs brought the stock from overvalued to roughly fair, not yet to bargain territory.
compact_checklist: Intuit sells subscription accounting software (QuickBooks, ~80% market share) and tax prep (TurboTax) to millions of small businesses and consumers, with ~80% gross margins and 75-80% recurring revenue.
QuickBooks' 80% US market share is protected by years of embedded financial data, 700+ third-party integrations, and a self-reinforcing accountant-client network effect that has held for two decades.
ROIC improved from 8.9% to 15.8% over four years, but $2.0B in annual SBC (51% of net income) drops SBC-adjusted ROIC to ~10-11% and real owner earnings yield to just 2.1%.
Competent operators who expanded operating margins from 20.1% to 26.2% in three years, but CEO Goodarzi has made zero open-market share purchases in five years while selling $26.6M in January 2026.
Net debt/EBITDA of 0.5x with 20x interest coverage and $4.6B cash means Intuit could service all debt for years even in a 30% revenue decline scenario.
At $447, the market requires ~18% annual growth in SBC-adjusted owner earnings for a decade — achievable only if AI monetization succeeds and SBC discipline emerges, neither of which is yet proven.
The business moat is genuine protection, but at 141% above conservative SBC-adjusted IV of $185, there is no price margin of safety — you are paying $2.42 for every $1 of conservative value.
Sell if SBC/revenue rises above 13% with no plan to moderate, or if revenue growth drops below 5% for two consecutive quarters signaling the compounding engine has stalled.
source_summary: Intuit FY2025 10-K, Q2 FY2026 earnings release (Feb 2026), SEC Form 4 filings, proxy statement, yfinance financial data, Morningstar moat analysis, QuickBooks market share data, IRS Direct File reports, Alpha Spread DCF, GuruFocus, Simply Wall St, analyst consensus estimates, Trefis, competitive landscape research
change_notes: Initial analysis. Watch verdict driven by high business quality (avg 7.1) offset by insufficient margin of safety (score 5) at current $447 price. SBC-adjusted valuation is the key differentiator vs consensus.
